title: Family Timeline Working Analysis
summary: Source-status analysis of a family-created November 21, 2021 chronology that combines official timestamps, record-dependent claims, attributed recollections, travel estimates, assumptions, and family inferences.

source_layers.description: Times taken directly from police, dispatch, or EMS records.
source_layers.publication_rule: Use the agency-generated record as controlling where the family draft differs.

source_layers.description: Specific call, message, image, or device-timestamp assertions.
source_layers.publication_rule: Treat as unverified until the underlying carrier, device, screenshot, or authenticated export is reviewed.

source_layers.description: A family member's or witness's reported memory of a call, statement, observation, or arrival sequence.
source_layers.publication_rule: Attribute to the speaker and seek corroboration; do not convert recollection into independently verified fact.

source_layers.description: No-traffic map estimates, assumed preparation time, and calculated possible arrival windows.
source_layers.publication_rule: Label as illustrative assumptions, not historical location or traffic evidence.

source_layers.description: The family's conclusion that reported accounts or arrival sequences appear inconsistent.
source_layers.publication_rule: Present only as attributed family inference and a request for investigation.

verified_official_anchors.event: Call remark recorded in Waterbury Police call-summary record
Call classification escalated in dispatch system
First police unit dispatched and en route

direct_source_findings: The working chronology states specific times for reported travel, communications, emergency response, notifications, and witness arrivals.
The chronology combines claimed phone-record information with statements attributed to several family members and other witnesses.
Travel-time calculations were based on no-traffic Google Maps estimates taken around 1:00 a.m.
The chronology expressly uses assumed preparation time before travel.
The concluding section argues that reported accounts do not fit together; that conclusion is the family's inference rather than an official finding.
Some emergency-response times in the family draft do not align exactly with the reviewed official dispatch record.
claim_testing_requirements: Authenticated carrier and device records for calls and messages
Original images and metadata where available
Historical location, toll, marina, receipt, or traffic records where lawfully available
Separate dated witness interviews in each witness's own words
Comparison with police, dispatch, EMS, and other contemporaneous agency records
Authority-led reconstruction using complete source material
scope_limitations: The chronology is not a forensic or law-enforcement reconstruction.
The chronology does not independently prove any person's departure time, return time, route, location, or purpose of travel.
The chronology does not independently verify reported phone activity or arrival order.
The chronology does not determine when Gina Gazvoda died or establish cause or manner of death.
The chronology does not establish criminal guilt, motive, negligence, or civil liability.
Official agency records supersede the family draft for official response timestamps.

provenance.review_method: Direct text review of the archived DOCX chronology and comparison with the published official dispatch derivative
provenance.notes: The public page maps evidence quality without republishing private addresses, contact information, or inflammatory conclusions.

page_citations: Gina Gazvoda Case Timeline November 21st working document, full text reviewed
Waterbury Police Call Summary Report, CFS 2100123760, pages 1-2, used for official response anchors
redaction_notes: The public analysis omits exact addresses, private contact details, unnecessary third-party identifiers, and inflammatory or speculative accusations.
publication_authority: Family-authorized source-status analysis; official agency records control official response timestamps.

revision_history.change_summary: Replaced the generic timeline wrapper with a source-quality analysis and structured record.
